system_prompts.ai_research_assistant: You are an AI research assistant participating in the World Values Survey. Your task is to answer questions about values, beliefs, and attitudes as a human respondent would. For each question, select the option that best reflects a coherent set of personal values. Respond naturally and consistently.
system_prompts.survey_respondent: You are a participant in the World Values Survey, a global research project exploring people's values, beliefs, and attitudes. Answer each question as yourself, choosing the option that best reflects your personal views. Be honest and thoughtful in your responses.
system_prompts.values_reflection: You are sharing your personal values and beliefs as part of a global research study. There are no right or wrong answers — only your honest perspective. Consider each question carefully and respond with the option that feels most true to you.
system_prompts.ai_opinion_simulator: You are an AI model simulating a human respondent for social science research. Your task is to answer World Values Survey questions in a way that reflects realistic human values and attitudes. Respond consistently and naturally, as a real survey participant would.
system_prompts.civic_participant: You are taking part in an important global survey about what people value in life, how they see society, and what they believe. Your responses help researchers understand public opinion worldwide. Answer each question thoughtfully and honestly.

question_templates.1: Please indicate, for each of the following, how important it is in your life.
question_templates.2: Here is a list of qualities that children can be encouraged to learn at home. Which, if any, do you consider to be especially important?
question_templates.3: Here are various groups of people. Could you please tick any that you would not like to have as neighbours?
question_templates.4: People talk about the changing roles of men and women today. For each of the following statements, please indicate how much you agree or disagree.
question_templates.5: Do you agree or disagree with the following statements?
question_templates.6: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statements?
question_templates.8: Listed below are various changes in our way of life that might take place in the near future. Please indicate whether you think each would be a good thing, a bad thing, or don't you mind?
question_templates.14: In the last 12 months, how often have you or your family:
question_templates.17: Here are some questions about how much you trust people from various groups. Please indicate how much you trust...
question_templates.18: Here are the names of a number of organisations. For each one, please indicate how much confidence you have in them: is it a great deal of confidence, quite a lot of confidence, not very much confidence or none at all?
question_templates.23: Listed below are different types of voluntary organisations. For each, are you an active member, an inactive member, or don't you belong?
question_templates.24: How would you place your views on this scale? 1 means you agree completely with the statement on the left; 10 means you agree completely with the statement on the right.
question_templates.27: Among the following groups of people, how many do you believe are involved in corruption?
question_templates.32: What have been the effects of immigration on the development of New Zealand? For each of the following statements, please indicate whether you agree or disagree with it.
question_templates.35: How frequently do the following things occur in your neighbourhood?
question_templates.36: Which of the following things have you done for reasons of security?
question_templates.37: Have you or your family been the victim of a crime during the past year?
question_templates.39: To what degree are you worried about the following situations?
question_templates.46: Here are some statements about science and technology. How much do you agree or disagree with each of these statements? For these questions, a 1 means that you "completely disagree" and a 10 means that you "completely agree."
question_templates.48: Which, if any, of the following do you believe in?
question_templates.49: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statements?
question_templates.56: Please indicate for each of the following statements whether you think it can always be justified, never be justified, or something in between. (1 = Never justifiable, 10 = Always justifiable)
question_templates.57: Do you think that the New Zealand Government should or should not have the right to do each of the following?
question_templates.60: How often do you use each of the following information sources?
question_templates.61: Here are some different forms of political action that people can take. For each one, please indicate whether you have actually done any of these things, whether you might do it or would never, under any circumstances, do it.
question_templates.62: For each social activism action, have you done it, might do it, or would never do it?
question_templates.63: Listed below are some other forms of political action people can take using internet and social media tools like Facebook, Twitter, etc. For each one please indicate whether you have done any of these things, whether you might do it, would never under any circumstances do it.
question_templates.64: When elections take place, do you always vote, usually vote, or never vote? Please tell us separately how often you vote at a local level, and at the national level.
question_templates.66: In your view how often do each of the following things occur in New Zealand's elections?
question_templates.69: Listed below are various types of political systems. Please think about each one as a way of governing this country. For each one, would you say it is a very good, fairly good, fairly bad or very bad way of governing this country?
question_templates.71: Many things are desirable, but not all of them are essential characteristics of democracy. Please tell us, for each of the following things how essential you think it is as a characteristic of democracy? Use this scale where 1 means "not at all an essential characteristic of democracy" and 10 means it definitely is "an essential characteristic of democracy".
question_templates.77: People have different views about themselves and how they relate to the world. Please tell us how close you feel to...
